channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Concerns over Canada's digital sovereignty and innovation in upcoming CUSMA renegotiations
item.description: In an opinion piece, John Ivison highlights growing concerns about Canada's position in the upcoming renegotiation of the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA). Canada's chief trade negotiator...

item.title: Global oil supply disrupted as Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure trigger energy crisis warnings
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency (IEA), Fatih Birol, has warned that the ongoing conflict involving Iran has removed around 13 million barrels of oil per day from global supply, creating wh...

item.title: Autopsy reveals 14‑year‑old Celeste Rivas Hernandez died from multiple penetrating injuries in D4vd homicide case
item.description: A newly released autopsy report shows that 14‑year‑old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the alleged murder victim of singer D4vd (real name David Anthony Burke), died from multiple penetrating injuries caused...

item.title: ABC reports rise in food relief demand, Canavan makes waves in politics, and Farrer seat battle intensifies
item.description: In a recent report by the ABC, a startling claim was made that over one-third of Australians are seeking food relief for the first time. This figure, derived from Oz Harvest’s annual Frontline Report,...

item.title: Head-on collision between two local trains in northern Denmark leaves multiple injured
item.description: A serious rail accident occurred in northern Denmark when two local trains collided head-on between the towns of Hillerød and Kagerup, approximately 40 kilometres north of Copenhagen. Authorities conf...

item.title: BBC investigation uncovers open drug sales in West Midlands mini-marts
item.description: A BBC investigation has revealed that illegal drugs such as cannabis and cocaine are being openly sold in some High Street mini-marts in towns across the West Midlands. Undercover researchers filmed i...

item.title: Reports Reveal Widespread Abuse and Lack of Care During Childbirth in DR Congo
item.description: For Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), many women don begin speak out about serious abuse and suffering wey dem face during childbirth for hospitals. Dis matter come to public eye after one viral vid...

item.title: Farmers adjust hay harvest timing to protect nesting threatened bird species
item.description: Some farmers are choosing to delay their annual hay harvest in an effort to protect threatened grassland bird species during their nesting season. In many parts of Canada, birds such as bobolinks and ...

item.title: Comelec mandates birth certificate submission for candidates in BSKE and upcoming elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that all candidates filing their certificates of candidacy (COC) for the 2026 Barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan Elections (BSKE), as well as future elec...

item.title: Karnataka SSLC 2026 Results Announcement: KSEAB to Declare Class 10 Scores Today at 12 PM
item.description: The Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB) is scheduled to declare the SSLC or Class 10 results for the year 2026 today on 23rd April 2026 at 12 PM. More than 8.65 lakh students who...

item.title: Thunder take 2-0 series lead as Gilgeous-Alexander powers past Suns in Game 2
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander delivered a standout performance with 37 points and nine assists to lead the Oklahoma City Thunder to a 120-107 win over the Phoenix Suns, giving his team a 2-0 advantage in th...

item.title: NDIS reforms and funding pressures: Mark Butler discusses proposed participant changes and cost sustainability
item.description: Nearly 15 years after the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) was established under the Gillard government, the Albanese government has announced what are being described as its most significa...

item.title: Eurozone Economy Slows as Middle East War Pushes Energy Costs and Disrupts Trade
item.description: Business activity for the eurozone don shrink for April, marking the first time in about 16 months wey things go down instead of grow. According to the Flash Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) wey S&P G...

item.title: Ballarat Trotting Club boosts bookings with ACM multi-channel marketing
item.description: The Ballarat and District Trotting Club partnered with Australian Community Media (ACM) to adopt a multi-channel marketing strategy aimed at attracting younger families and overcoming the perception t...

item.title: Borno families beg government to rescue 416 hostages as terrorists give 72-hour ransom ultimatum
item.description: Some families for Ngoshe community inside Gwoza Local Government Area of Borno State dey still dey cry since March 3 when Boko Haram/ISWAP terrorists invade their town and carry 416 people, mostly wom...

item.title: PNP reviews police operation after 12-year-old girl killed during GenSan encounter
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has launched an investigation into the death of a 12-year-old girl who was killed during an alleged shootout between police officers and a suspect in General Santo...

item.title: Starmer calls for unity and condemns hate incidents in St George’s Day message
item.description: In a video message released to mark St George’s Day on 22 April, UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer warned about the growing presence of divisive influences both within the country and internationally...

item.title: EAC Launches Regional Plan Discussions to Strengthen Agriculture and Food Systems
item.description: The East African Community (EAC), working together with AGRA, has started a high-level meeting in Dar es Salaam to review and validate the Draft Regional Agri-Food Systems Investment Plan (RASIP) for ...

item.title: SWAN Pays Tribute to Former NFF President Ibrahim Galadima After His Death
item.description: Sports Writers Association of Nigeria (SWAN) don express deep sorrow over the death of former Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) President, Alhaji Ibrahim Galadima, wey pass on after long illness. Gala...

item.title: BBC investigation reveals illegal drug sales in West Midlands high street mini-marts
item.description: A BBC undercover investigation has exposed the sale of illegal drugs including cocaine, cannabis, nitrous oxide and prescription medicines in mini-marts on high streets in the West Midlands. Secret fi...
